question: What are the college timings and working hours?
answer: College timings are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays. Administrative offices close at 4:30 PM on Saturdays.
answer_template: College timings are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays. Administrative offices close at 4:30 PM on Saturdays.

question: What is the fee structure and payment schedule?
answer: Fees vary by program (B.Tech, MBA, etc.). Tuition is typically payable per semester via online portal or demand draft. Late fees apply after the due date shown on the portal.
answer_template: Fees vary by program. For the {department} department, tuition for semester {semester} is payable via the online portal or demand draft. Late fees apply after the due date.

question: When are semester exams and how is the exam schedule announced?
answer: Semester exams are held at the end of each semester. Provisional exam dates are published on the institute portal at least two weeks in advance; final dates appear on the official timetable.
answer_template: Semester {semester} exams for {department} are held at the end of the semester. Provisional dates are published on the institute portal at least two weeks in advance.

question: How can I apply for admissions and what is the process?
answer: Apply online through the college admissions portal, upload required documents, pay the application fee, and track status via the same dashboard. Entrance test or merit criteria depend on the program.
answer_template: Apply online through the college admissions portal for {department}. Upload required documents, pay the application fee, and track your status via the dashboard.

question: Are scholarships available for students?
answer: Yes. Merit-based and need-based scholarships are offered subject to eligibility. Details and deadlines are listed under the Scholarships section on the official website each academic year.
answer_template: Yes. Merit-based and need-based scholarships are available for {department} students. Details and deadlines are listed under the Scholarships section on the official website.

question: Does the college provide hostel accommodation?
answer: Hostel facilities are available for eligible students on a first-come, first-served basis. Apply through the hostel office after admission confirmation; fees include room and mess charges separately.
answer_template: Hostel facilities are available for eligible students. Apply through the hostel office after admission confirmation; fees include room and mess charges separately.

question: How do I contact the college office or departments?
answer: You can reach the main office at office@college.edu or call the reception listed on the Contact Us page. Department HOD emails and phone extensions are also published on the portal.
answer_template: You can reach the {department} department or main office at office@college.edu. Department HOD emails and phone extensions are published on the portal.

question: What is the academic holiday list?
answer: National holidays, institute-declared breaks, and term vacations are published in the annual academic calendar on the website. Updates are emailed to all registered students.
answer_template: National holidays, institute-declared breaks, and term vacations are published in the annual academic calendar. Updates are emailed to all registered students.

question: What are the placement opportunities and training support?
answer: The Training and Placement Cell coordinates campus drives, internships, and soft-skills workshops. Eligible final-year students register on the placement portal; companies and dates are announced there.
answer_template: The Training and Placement Cell coordinates campus drives and internships for {department} students. Register on the placement portal; companies and dates are announced there.

question: Which academic departments and programs are offered?
answer: The institute offers undergraduate and postgraduate programs across departments such as Computer Science, Information Technology, Mechanical, Civil, Electronics, and Management Studies.
answer_template: The institute offers undergraduate and postgraduate programs across departments such as Computer Science, Information Technology, Mechanical, Civil, Electronics, and Management Studies.

question: What are library hours and borrowing rules?
answer: The central library is open on all working days from 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM. Borrowing limits and renewal rules are printed on your library card and the digital library portal.
answer_template: The central library is open from 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM on all working days. Borrowing limits and renewal rules are printed on your library card and the digital library portal.

question: Where can I find my class timetable?
answer: Class timetables are published on the student portal under Timetable. Department notice boards may show updates; contact your class coordinator for clashes or changes.
answer_template: Class timetable for semester {semester} ({department} dept.) is published on the student portal. Contact your class coordinator for clashes or changes.

question: Where is the campus located?
answer: The campus is located in Nagpur (Wathoda area). Full address, maps, and public transport options are on the Visit Us page of the college website.
answer_template: The campus is located in Nagpur (Wathoda area). Full address, maps, and public transport options are on the Visit Us page of the college website.

question: What is the minimum attendance requirement?
answer: Students are generally required to maintain at least 75% attendance as per university norms, unless exempted under documented medical or approved leave policies.
answer_template: Students in semester {semester} must maintain at least 75% attendance per university norms, unless exempted under documented medical or approved leave policies.

question: Is college bus or transport facility available?
answer: A limited bus service covers major routes in the city. Routes, timings, and fees are announced at the start of each semester by the transport office.
answer_template: A limited bus service covers major routes in the city. Routes, timings, and fees are announced at the start of each semester by the transport office.
